# Implementation Plan: Action Architecture v2

**Branch**: `005-action-architecture-v2` | **Date**: 2026-05-07 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from [`specs/005-action-architecture-v2/spec.md`](./spec.md)
**Generated by**: `speckit-plan` orchestrator after `speckit-clarify` PASS-WITH-CAVEATS verdict.

---

## Summary

Replace the ad-hoc `ActionRequest` sealed class — currently mixing WhatsApp specifics (legacy spec 002) with generic open-app dispatch — with a single domain `Action` type carrying `(schemaVersion, providerId, payload, fallback)`. Introduce port-shaped `ActionDispatcher` and `ProviderRegistry` in `commonMain`; implement Android adapter with one handler per provider (whatsapp, telegram, phone, sms, browser, youtube, app, system-settings) and a debounced provider-availability stream backed by `AppIndex`. Delete all spec 002 residue (10 files); cleanup orphaned `SharedPreferences`. Wire format ships with `schemaVersion: 1` and a single-function legacy migration that is fitness-function-gated to expire in spec 006.

This spec adds **no new external dependencies**, **no new runtime permissions**, and **no new background work**. APK delta is 0 KB.

---

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Kotlin 2.0.21 (KMP), JDK 17, Android Gradle Plugin 8.7.3 (inherited from spec 004).
**Primary Dependencies**: Compose Multiplatform 1.6+, kotlinx-serialization-json (already present), kotlinx-coroutines-core, Decompose, Koin (all from spec 004 stack). **No new deps.**
**Storage**: One-time SharedPreferences cleanup of `launcher.communication.return_context` (deletion only, no new keys per Clarification C1 wire-format checklist follow-up).
**Testing**: JUnit5 + kotlin.test for `commonTest`; Robolectric (already present from spec 004) for Android handler tests via `Intent.filterEquals`. Konsist for fitness function §8.1. Compose UI testing for `AddSlotWizardScreen` provider-list filtering.
**Target Platform**: Android `minSdk 26`, `targetSdk 35`, `compileSdk 35`. iOS source-set compiles empty (no implementations this spec).
**Project Type**: Multi-module KMP launcher (`:app` + `:core`).
**Performance Goals**: Cold start `HomeActivity` ≤ 600ms (inherited from 004 baseline, must not regress). Dispatch latency ≤ 50ms with fake registry. Registry update flow ≤ 1 emit/sec (Clarification C3).
**Constraints**: No new permissions; no new background work; intent dispatch only on user-initiated actions; PII never logged (`ActionDispatched` event whitelist).
**Scale/Scope**: 8 handlers (7 providers + system-settings), ~14 new files in `:core`, ~10 file deletions, 3 mock-JSON rewrites, 1 contract document, 1 diagnostics-events document.

---

## Architecture

### Module shape

**No new gradle modules in this spec.** All code lives in `:core`.

**Обоснование** против выделения handlers + dispatcher + registry в отдельный `:feature-actions` модуль (анализ по Article V §3 конституции):

| Критерий Article V §3 | Применимо к action-architecture? |
|-----------------------|----------------------------------|
| Clear ownership boundary | ❌ всё в одной зоне ответственности |
| Build isolation | ⚠ возможна, но не требуется (без CI-узких мест) |
| Independent enable/disable | ❌ handlers всегда включены |
| Stable API | ✓ `ActionDispatcher`, `ProviderRegistry` стабильны |
| Materially better testability | ⚠ тесты уже изолированы через `commonTest` fakes |
| Release separation | ❌ нет |

1.5 из 6 — формально модуль допустим (✓ stable API), но Article V §160 («favour a small, disciplined module graph over micro-modules») перевешивает. Сейчас в проекте 2 модуля (`:app` + `:core`), [`settings.gradle.kts`](../../settings.gradle.kts) предусматривает паттерн `:feature-*` для будущих, но без планов на ближайший спек.

**Exit ramp**: когда появится 2-й feature-модуль (например, `:feature-favorites` в spec 006/008), выделить handlers + dispatcher + registry в `:feature-actions`. Цена: `git mv` файлов из `:core/androidMain/com/launcher/core/actions/` + `include(":feature-actions")` + правки imports. Оценка: ≤ 1 рабочий день. Wire format `Action` (commonMain в `:core/api/`) **остаётся в `:core`** независимо от выделения handler'ов — это публичный контракт, не реализация.

**Решение**: в spec 005 — оставить в `:core`. Не выделять модуль ради одного владельца.

```
:core
├── commonMain/kotlin/com/launcher/api/         ← ports + domain types (NEW)
│   ├── Action.kt                                ← Action, ActionPayload, ProviderId, DispatchResult, UnavailabilityHint
│   ├── ActionDispatcher.kt                      ← interface (port)
│   ├── ProviderRegistry.kt                      ← interface (port) + ProviderState, ProviderAvailability
│   └── ActionWireFormat.kt                      ← kotlinx-serialization wiring + migrateLegacyAction
├── commonMain/kotlin/com/launcher/api/          ← TO DELETE
│   ├── CommunicationModels.kt                   ← X
│   └── ActionModels.kt                          ← X (replaced by Action.kt)
├── androidMain/kotlin/com/launcher/core/actions/
│   ├── AndroidActionDispatcher.kt               ← NEW (replaces ActionDispatcher.kt)
│   ├── handlers/                                ← NEW directory
│   │   ├── ActionHandler.kt                     ← interface
│   │   ├── AppLaunchHandler.kt
│   │   ├── WhatsAppHandler.kt
│   │   ├── TelegramHandler.kt
│   │   ├── PhoneHandler.kt
│   │   ├── SmsHandler.kt
│   │   ├── BrowserHandler.kt
│   │   ├── YouTubeHandler.kt
│   │   └── SystemSettingsHandler.kt
│   ├── PlayStoreFallbackResolver.kt             ← NEW
│   ├── CustomPayloadValidator.kt                ← NEW (security CHK-011)
│   ├── ActionDispatcher.kt                      ← TO DELETE (replaced)
│   ├── CommunicationConfigValidator.kt          ← TO DELETE
│   ├── WhatsAppLaunchabilityResolver.kt         ← TO DELETE
│   └── ReturnContextStore.kt                    ← TO DELETE (cleanup before delete)
├── androidMain/kotlin/com/launcher/core/providers/
│   └── AndroidProviderRegistry.kt               ← NEW
├── commonMain/kotlin/com/launcher/core/actions/
│   ├── ActionCycleGuard.kt                      ← TO DELETE
│   └── RestoreOutcomeEvaluator.kt               ← TO DELETE
├── commonMain/kotlin/com/launcher/core/events/
│   └── CommunicationDiagnostics.kt              ← TO DELETE (replaced by ActionDispatched event in EventRouter)
├── androidMain/assets/
│   ├── flows_mock_workspace.json                ← REWRITE (new wire format)
│   ├── flows_mock_simple-launcher.json          ← REWRITE
│   ├── flows_mock_launcher.json                 ← REWRITE
│   ├── whatsapp_tiles_mock.json                 ← TO DELETE (content moves to mock_contacts.json)
│   └── mock_contacts.json                       ← NEW (provider-agnostic contact records)
├── commonMain/composeResources/values/
│   └── strings_actions.xml                      ← NEW (provider names, unavailability, fallback prompts)
└── commonTest/resources/fixtures/action-wire-format/  ← NEW (Clarification C4)
    ├── whatsapp-message-v1.json
    ├── phone-call-v1.json
    ├── ... (one per ActionPayload variant)
    └── legacy-spec003-whatsapp-call.json        ← backward-compat fixture
```

### Port-adapter shape

```
                                 commonMain
   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   │  Action (data) ─── ActionDispatcher (port) ─── ProviderRegistry (port)
   │       ▲                    ▲                          ▲
   └───────┼────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼───
           │                    │                          │
           │             FakeActionDispatcher        FakeProviderRegistry  ← commonTest
           │                                                │
           │  androidMain                                   │
   ┌───────┴────────────────────┴──────────────────────────┴────┐
   │  AndroidActionDispatcher ── AndroidProviderRegistry         │
   │           │                          │                       │
   │  ┌────────┴───────┐          uses AppIndex.snapshot          │
   │  │  Map<Provider  │          (debounced 1s, distinct)        │
   │  │  Id, Handler>  │                                          │
   │  └────────────────┘                                          │
   │     8 handler classes                                        │
   └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Data flow on `dispatch()`

```
User taps tile
   ↓
FlowScreen → dispatchAction: (Action) → DispatchResult  (Compose state)
   ↓
RootComponent.dispatch  (commonMain, port)
   ↓
AndroidActionDispatcher.dispatch  (androidMain, impl)
   ↓
1. validate schemaVersion
2. lookup handler by providerId (unknown → fallback or ProviderUnavailable)
3. check ProviderRegistry.availability (Missing/NotApplicable → fallback)
4. handler.handle(action) → builds Intent, calls context.startActivity
5. on Failure & fallback present → recurse (depth ≤ 2)
6. emit ProjectEvent.ActionDispatched(providerId, result, fallbackUsed)
   ↓
DispatchResult ↑ back to UI for snackbar feedback (only on Failure / ProviderUnavailable)
```

---

## Data model

See [`data-model.md`](./data-model.md). Summary:

- `Action` — wire-format root; carries `schemaVersion`, `providerId`, `payload`, `fallback`, `sourceModuleId`.
- `ProviderId` — `value class` over `String`; constants for 8 known providers (Clarification C1: forward-compat).
- `ActionPayload` — sealed class with 9 variants + `Custom(key, params: Map<String, String>)` (Clarification C2).
- `DispatchResult` — sealed class: `Ok`, `BlockedByPolicy`, `ProviderUnavailable(providerId, hint)`, `Failure(reason)`.
- `UnavailabilityHint` — enum: `Missing`, `NotApplicable`, `UnknownInThisVersion`.
- `ProviderState`, `ProviderAvailability`, `InstallHint` — registry types.

---

## Wire formats

See [`contracts/`](./contracts/) for full per-format docs.

| Contract | File | Version | Used by |
|----------|------|---------|---------|
| Action wire format | [`action-wire-format.md`](./contracts/action-wire-format.md) | 1.0.0 | Mock JSON assets, future backend sync (spec 007), future QR share (spec 010). |
| Diagnostics events v2 | [`contracts/diagnostics-events-v2.md`](./contracts/diagnostics-events-v2.md) | 2.0.0 | `EventRouter` consumers; replaces `CommunicationDiagnostics` from spec 002. |

---

## Dependency impact

**No new gradle dependencies.** All required APIs (kotlinx-serialization, Compose, Decompose, Koin) inherited from spec 004 stack per ADR-005.

**APK delta**: 0 KB (no new artifacts; net negative due to spec 002 file deletions, ~3 KB).

---

## Test strategy

Per CLAUDE.md rule §6 (mock-first) and §7 (fitness functions where feasible), and per Article X (test order: contract → integration → unit → UI):

### Contract tests (commonTest)

- `ActionWireFormatTest`:
  - `roundtrip_each_payload_variant_v1` — every `ActionPayload` variant (including `Custom` with empty/full params, including `null` fallback, including 2-deep fallback chain).
  - `backward_compat_spec003_legacy` — for each fixture in `commonTest/resources/fixtures/action-wire-format/legacy-*.json`, parses via `migrateLegacyAction`, asserts target `Action` equality.
  - `forward_compat_unknown_provider` — JSON with `providerId: "future-thing"` parses successfully (does not throw).
  - `unsupported_schema_version` — JSON with `schemaVersion: 99` returns `Failure("unsupported schema")` from dispatcher.

### Fake-adapter tests (commonTest)

- `FakeActionDispatcherTest` — records actions, returns programmable `DispatchResult`, verifies fallback-chain traversal logic (depth ≤ 2 enforcement).
- `FakeProviderRegistryTest` — programmable availability, verifies UI filtering (consumed by `AddSlotWizardScreenTest`).

### Android handler tests (androidUnitTest, Robolectric)

For each handler — one test class:
- `WhatsAppHandlerTest` — `Intent.filterEquals` against expected `Intent(ACTION_VIEW, "https://wa.me/<contact>")` with `setPackage("com.whatsapp")`.
- `PhoneHandlerTest` — `Intent.filterEquals` against `Intent(ACTION_DIAL, "tel:<number>")`. **No `ACTION_CALL`** assertion (security CHK-015).
- `SmsHandlerTest` — `Intent(ACTION_SENDTO, "smsto:<number>")` with optional body.
- `BrowserHandlerTest` — `Intent(ACTION_VIEW, "https://...")`, no `setPackage`.
- `YouTubeHandlerTest` — `vnd.youtube://...` first; web fallback path tested separately.
- `TelegramHandlerTest`, `AppLaunchHandlerTest`, `SystemSettingsHandlerTest` — same pattern.
- `PlayStoreFallbackResolverTest` — `market://details?id=X` + web `https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=X` fallback.
- `CustomPayloadValidatorTest` — security CHK-011: rejects > 16 keys, key length > 64, value length > 1024.

### Integration tests (androidUnitTest)

- `AndroidActionDispatcherIntegrationTest` — wires real handlers + `FakeProviderRegistry`; verifies fallback chain end-to-end with `ActionPayload.WhatsAppMessage → fallback OpenApp(com.whatsapp) → Play Store`.

### UI tests (androidUnitTest, Compose)

- `AddSlotWizardScreenTest` — provider list filters by `FakeProviderRegistry`; "missing" shows install hint; "unknown-in-version" hidden.
- `FlowScreenDispatchTest` — tile tap → calls `dispatchAction` with correct `Action`; double-tap within 500ms → second tap suppressed (UI debounce per §7.6).
- `ConfirmationOverlayTest` — `DispatchResult.Failure` shows snackbar with retry action (resolves ux-quality CHK-020).

### Fitness functions (CI)

Per spec §8 + Clarification C5:

1. **Konsist domain-isolation** (`domainIsolationTest`): no `android.*`, `androidx.*`, `*.intent.*`, `*.content.Context`, `*.net.Uri` imports in `core/src/commonMain/`.
2. **Action wire-format roundtrip** (above) — failing the roundtrip blocks PR.
3. **WhatsApp residue gate** (`whatsappResidueTest`): grep across `core/src/`, `app/src/`, `docs/dev/` for forbidden symbols (`WhatsAppHandoff`, `ReturnContext`, `ActionCycleGuard`, `CommunicationConfigValidator`, `WhatsAppLaunchabilityResolver`, `RestoreOutcomeEvaluator`, `CommunicationDiagnostics`) → must yield 0 hits.
4. **Legacy-bridge expiry gate** (`legacyMigrationExpiryTest`): if `MIGRATE_LEGACY_ACTION_DEADLINE_SPEC` constant in `build.gradle.kts` ≤ latest merged spec id, symbol `migrateLegacyAction` must not exist in `core/src/commonMain/`.

---

## Risks

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|------------|--------|------------|
| WhatsApp deep-link format changes (vendor controls `wa.me`/`whatsapp://`) | Low/Medium | Whatsapp dispatch breaks for active users | `WhatsAppHandler` wrapped via `androidMain` adapter (CLAUDE.md §2 ACL); change isolated to one file. Health metric in spec 006 will surface increased Failure rate. |
| OEM `<queries>` enforcement on Android 11+ — without entries, `getInstalledApplications` returns empty, `AppIndex` reports nothing | High if missed | All providers report `Missing` → users see "not installed" everywhere | Manifest `<queries>` entries for known package names (whatsapp, whatsapp.w4b, telegram, telegram.plus, youtube, youtube.tv) **planned as task in `tasks.md`**; permissions-platform CHK-008 traced. |
| `Custom.params` accepted from external future config (spec 007 backend) without server-side validation → DoS via huge payload | Low (spec 007 not yet built) | Parcel size exhaustion on `Intent.putExtras` | `CustomPayloadValidator` (security CHK-011) — bounds enforced at `Action` parse time, not at dispatch. |
| `migrateLegacyAction` bridge persists past spec 006 (mission creep) | Medium (humans forget) | Legacy code becomes load-bearing again | Fitness function §8.4 (Clarification C5): CI fails if function still present after deadline. Forces explicit slip-decision in PR. |
| Cold-start regression from new Koin registrations (8 handlers + registry + validator + resolver) | Medium | Misses `HomeActivity ≤ 600ms` ADR-005 budget | Lazy registration: only `AppLaunchHandler` + `SystemSettingsHandler` eager (covered by typical first-launch flows); rest lazy. Perf-checkpoint task validates. |
| Compose UI debounce (§7.6) inconsistent across all action surfaces | Low | Double-dispatch in some places | `TileCard`/`PrimaryActionButton` modifier `clickableDebounced(500ms)` factored as single composable; ux-quality CHK-011 traced. |
| iOS source-set lacks any provider implementation; future iOS spec finds asymmetry | Certain | iOS dispatch when ready will need full reimplementation per provider | Documented Platform Asymmetry per ADR-005 §3 Gate 2. Acceptable; iOS work is its own spec. |

## Constitution Check

*Run via `procedure-constitution-check` against this plan, 2026-05-07.*

### Gate 1 — Architecture

| Question | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Layered architecture or justified deviation? | **PASS.** UI in `:core/commonMain/ui/`, ports in `:core/commonMain/api/`, adapters in `:core/androidMain/`. No layering violation. |
| New module justified per Article V §3? | **PASS — N/A.** No new gradle module. |
| Boundaries explicit and minimal? | **PASS.** 2 ports (`ActionDispatcher`, `ProviderRegistry`), each consumed by named callers; handler-registry is internal to `AndroidActionDispatcher`. |

### Gate 2 — Core/System Integration

| Question | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Feature requires system events? | **PASS — Yes.** Package install/uninstall events for `ProviderRegistry` updates. |
| Centralised in Core? | **PASS.** Reuses existing `AppIndex` + `EventRouter` from spec 003/004; no new feature-side broadcast registration. |
| Event contracts typed and documented? | **PASS.** [`contracts/diagnostics-events-v2.md`](./contracts/diagnostics-events-v2.md) defines `ActionDispatched` event taxonomy. Article VI §6 fields (source/frequency/threading/battery cost/fallback) documented for `ProviderRegistry.updates`. |

### Gate 3 — Configuration

| Question | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Affects profiles/schema/defaults/migrations? | **PASS — Yes (wire format).** `Action` is new; `flows_mock_*.json` rewritten; `whatsapp_tiles_mock.json` removed. |
| Validation/compatibility covered per Article VII §3? | **PASS.** Versioning (`schemaVersion: 1`), validation (`schemaVersion > SUPPORTED` → Failure), backward-compat (`migrateLegacyAction`), defaults (`fallback = null`), migration policy (legacy bridge with expiry — Clarification C5). |
| Persisted/wire-format change carries `schemaVersion`? | **PASS.** `Action` schemaVersion required from first commit. |

### Gate 4 — Required Context Review

| Question | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Relevant `docs/**` files identified? | **PASS.** 9 files reviewed (above). |
| Linked in plan? | **PASS.** All as markdown links. |
| Omissions explained? | **PASS.** ADR-002, ADR-003, market/subscription research explicitly noted as N/A. |

### Gate 5 — Accessibility

| Question | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Behaviour for elderly + a11y users? | **PASS.** Inherits 56dp tap-target, 18sp body, ≥4.5:1 contrast from spec 004; new `AddSlotWizardScreen` provider items use same `PrimaryActionButton` composable. |
| Acceptance criteria? | **PASS.** US-507 acceptance includes "shown / hidden / install-hint" for each availability state, all using existing accessible composables. |
| Tap target / contrast / contentDescription / TalkBack path? | **PASS.** Inherited; new provider names have explicit `contentDescription = stringResource(provider_name_*)`. |

### Gate 6 — Battery/Performance

| Question | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Background/runtime cost? | **PASS.** No new background work. `ProviderRegistry.updates` is reactive on existing `AppIndex.snapshot`, not a new poller. |
| Polling avoided? | **PASS.** Event-driven via `AppIndex` package-set-changed event; debounced 1s per Clarification C3. |
| Startup impact controlled? | **PASS.** Lazy handler registration in Koin; only 2 eager (AppLaunch, SystemSettings). Perf-checkpoint task verifies ADR-005 cold-start budget. |
| Measurable targets present? | **PASS.** §9 NFR table: dispatch ≤ 50ms, registry update rate ≤ 1/sec, cold start ≤ 600ms. |

### Gate 7 — Testing

| Question | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Contract / integration / regression / UI tests planned? | **PASS.** §Test Strategy lists all four categories. |
| Wire-format roundtrip + backward-compat? | **PASS.** `ActionWireFormatTest` covers both. |
| Core event handling tests (translation/dedup/throttling/fallback)? | **PASS.** `FakeProviderRegistryTest` verifies debounce; integration test verifies fallback chain. |
| Per CLAUDE.md §6 — every port has fake adapter + real adapter + DI wiring? | **PASS.** `FakeActionDispatcher`, `FakeProviderRegistry` in `commonTest`; `Android*` real impls in `androidMain`; Koin wiring in `PlatformKoinModule.android.kt`. |

### Gate 8 — Simplicity

| Question | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Speculative abstractions? | **PASS.** Handler-registry justified by 8 implementations (>3 — clear win over big `when`). `ActionDispatcher` interface justified by fake adapter + future iOS impl. `PlayStoreFallbackResolver` is a class (not extension function) for DI testability — meta-minimization CHK-002 confirmed. |
| Design reducible further? | **PASS.** `ActionPayload` could be `Map<String, JsonElement>` (untyped) — rejected (loses type safety). `Action` could be flat record — rejected (forward-compat for new payload variants requires sealed). Current shape is minimum-viable. |
| CLAUDE.md §4 Test 1 / Test 2? | **PASS.** Documented per decision in spec §5.1–5.5. |

### Verdict

**8/8 PASS** — plan ready for `speckit-tasks`.

## Plan-level checklist re-runs

Per `speckit-plan` Step 5 — re-run on plan-level concerns:

- `checklist-domain-isolation` — verified above (Gate 1, Gate 7); no plan-level new concerns.
- `checklist-wire-format` — wire-format checklist open items CHK-013 (no new SharedPreferences keys) ✓ explicitly stated; CHK-018 (`contracts/action-wire-format.md`) ✓ produced this pass.
- `checklist-meta-minimization` — meta-minimization CHK-002 (PlayStoreFallbackResolver) ✓ justified above (DI testability, not premature).

All clarify-time PASS-WITH-CAVEATS open items now resolved or queued as tasks.

---

## Rollout / verification

- **Implementation order**: foundation → domain types → wire format → fakes → handlers (parallel, one per provider) → registry → DI wiring → UI updates → fitness functions → cleanup → smoke. See `tasks.md`.
- **Performance verification**: `perf-checkpoint.md` produced after handler integration. Compares cold-start vs spec 004 baseline; documents dispatch latency on Pixel 4a emulator.
- **Smoke verification**: two-emulator run per `android-emulator` skill: workspace + simple-launcher presets; one whatsapp dispatch each; one phone dispatch each; one fallback-chain dispatch (whatsapp uninstalled → Play Store).
- **Doc updates**: `roadmap.md` marks spec 005 done; `permissions-and-resource-budget.md` adds spec-005 row (0 delta); `messenger-calling-research.md` cross-link from `WhatsAppHandler` source comment.
- **Done definition**: see [`spec.md` §13](./spec.md).

---

## Complexity Tracking

No Constitution Check violations. No exceptions to justify.

---

## TL;DR (по-русски, для новичка и для будущего AI)

**Суть.** Никаких новых gradle-модулей. Весь код в `:core`. В общем для всех платформ коде (`commonMain`) — типы (`Action`, `ActionDispatcher`, `ProviderRegistry`) и сериализация. В Android-части — `AndroidActionDispatcher` + 8 handler-классов в подпапке `handlers/` + `AndroidProviderRegistry` (наблюдатель за `AppIndex` с 1-секундным debounce). На iOS — пустая заглушка, чтобы код собирался. Constitution Check 8/8 PASS, никаких исключений.

**Конкретика, которую стоит запомнить:**
- ~14 новых файлов, ~10 удаляемых, 3 переписываемых JSON-моков, 1 новый `mock_contacts.json`, 1 новый `strings_actions.xml`, 2 контракта.
- `<queries>` в манифесте для `com.whatsapp`, `com.whatsapp.w4b`, `org.telegram.messenger`, `org.telegram.plus`, `com.google.android.youtube` + intent-фильтры для `tel:`, `smsto:`, `https:` (без них на Android 11+ программа не видит установленные приложения).
- Контракт «logging» жёсткий: ровно 4 поля в `ProjectEvent.ActionDispatched` (providerId, resultKind, fallbackUsed, timestampMs) — никаких номеров/контактов/URL никогда. Защищено автотестом «таксономия событий заморожена».
- В Koin: `AppLaunchHandler` + `SystemSettingsHandler` загружаются eager (нужны на первом старте), остальные 6 handler'ов — lazy (чтобы не тратить cold-start бюджет 600мс).
- Тесты: roundtrip JSON, backward-compat для spec 003 моков, по тесту на каждый handler через `Intent.filterEquals`, integration-тест fallback-цепочки, UI-тест `AddSlotWizardScreen`.

**На что смотреть с осторожностью:**
- Раздел Risks, строка 5 — регрессия cold-start при добавлении handler-регистраций; митигация = lazy-загрузка большинства, перф-чекпоинт обязателен.
- Раздел Risks, строка 2 — пропустить `<queries>` = `AppIndex` вернёт пустоту = все провайдеры покажутся «не установлено».
- Phase 6 удалений включает `LauncherCore.start()` cleanup `SharedPreferences` (T605) с grep-якорем; этот код нельзя удалять при будущих рефакторах, пока не пройдёт несколько релизов на устройствах пользователей.
- Module shape — обоснование «no new gradle module» проанализировано по Article V §3 (1.5/6 критериев). Exit ramp: выделить в `:feature-actions`, когда появится 2-й feature-модуль — оценка ≤ 1 рабочий день.
